name: WateROAM ROAMfilter Ultra System
description: This WateROAM ROAMfilter Ultra is a Ultra Filtration hollow-fibre water filtration system for community use.

taxonomy.sub_category: WateROAM ROAMfilter Ultra System Updated on January 2, 2024 · Created on June 7, 2016 WateROAM ROAMfilter Ultra System This WateROAM ROAMfilter Ultra is a Ultra Filtration hollow-fibre water filtration system for community use. Developed By wateroam Tested By Setsco Services Pte Ltd wateroam Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The WateROAM ROAMfilter Ultra System uses a submersible electronic pump and a .02μm pore size filter to effectively remove bacteria and viruses 99.9999% at a rate of 1500 L/hr. The product has been distributed and implemented across 44 countries since 2015, with further plans for global expansion. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being Market Suggested Retail Price $2,900.00 Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household, Public Sector Agencies, NGOs Distributors / Implementing Organizations WaterROAM has a number of implementing organizations. Organizations include: World Vision, Togather Cafe, Relief Singapore, Singapore Red Cross, Center for a Better Tomorrow. Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Aqua-Cura Solar , Living Water Treatment Systems , and GravityPure UF . Countries Cambodia, Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines Manufacturing/Building Method The product is mass-manufactured by WateROAM Ltd. Intellectural Property Type Patent User Provision Model Users can obtain the ROAMfilter Ultra System from the WateROAM website. The product is also distributed through WateROAM partners in-country. Distributions to Date Status Although the exact number of ROAMfilter Ultra Systems distributed to date is unknown, they have been distributed to countries in Southeast Asia through collaborations with aid organizations. As of 2019, WateROAM technologies had collectively been implemented in 33 countries reaching over 83,000 people. Target use case Household and community use Manufacturer-specified flow rate (L/hr) 1500 L / hr Bacteria reduction 7 log (Coliforms) Type of membrane filtration Ultra Filtration hollow-fibre Virus reduction 4 log Protozoa reduction 4 log (Cryptosporidium) Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction No Maximum recommended influent turbidity level (NTU) Unknown Effluent turbidity levels (NTU) Unknown Material of construction Hydrophilic modified PVDF Safe water storage capacity (L) None Manufacturer-specified lifetime volume (L) Unknown Design Specifications The ROAMfilter Ultra System filters 800 L/hr using hollow-fiber technologies with a pore size of 0.02 micron, and surface area of 480m2. The product can be gravity fed, requiring no electricity, or powered by a 400W submersible pump. The weight of the system is 45kg. Product Schematics Technical Support Technical support is provided by WateROAM. Replacement Components Replaceable components include the membrane filter, tubing, valves, and taps. Lifecycle The product has an expected lifespan of 2 years. Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters The ROAMfilter Ultra System is designed to filter up to 1500 L/hr removing sand, sediments, 99.9999% bacteria and 99.99% viruses. Vetted Performance Status Testing by Setsco determined a 6 log coliform reduction. Safety WateROAM’s products are not designed to remove compounds affecting taste such as soluble, chemicals and heavy metals. Complementary Technical Systems Safe water storage available through the manufacturer. Academic Research and References Pooi, C., Ng, H., 2018, Review of low-cost point-of-use water treatment systems for developing communities, NPJ: Clean Water. Vol 11. “ROAMfilter Ultra, potable water at a High flow rate,” WATEROAM. Available: https://www.wateroam.com/roamfilter-ultra.html “See Wateroam’s impact in reducing the shortage of water,” WATEROAM. Available: https://www.edgeanalytical.com/wp-content/uploads/Micro_SM9222.pdf/ Compliance with regulations The product complies with WHO safe drinking water guidelines as determined by testing by Setsco. Evaluation methods The manufacturer cites Coloiform reduction of 7 log as evaluation criteria, and 4 log bacteria. The product has been evaluated by third-party testing organization Setsco, using APHA 9222B evaluation. Other Information WateROAM has received various awards for their products.
